text.title: AI Governance and Compliance Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every governance pack enforces. Hard rules — regulator overrides only with documented derogation.

rule.statement: Every shipped high-risk system has a versioned model card with at least: provenance (training data summary, dates), intended use, out-of-scope use, evaluation metrics, fairness metrics, known limitations. Card is stored as code in the model repo and bumped on every retrain.

rationale.source: EU AI Act Article 11-13; Hugging Face model card spec; Google PAIR practice

rule.rationale: Undocumented systems cannot be audited; cards go stale within a release if not pinned to code.

rule.statement: Bias audit reports demographic parity difference, equalised odds, and disparate impact ratios against a stratified validation set. Each metric ships with a 95% CI. Industry default threshold: 0.1 demographic parity difference; tighter for credit / hiring / law enforcement.

rationale.source: EU AI Act Annex IV; ISO/IEC 23894; fairness ML literature (Verma+Rubin 2018)

rule.rationale: Naked claims of "we tested for bias" without statistical tests do not survive auditor review.

rule.statement: Explainability artefacts are typed: per-decision (SHAP/LIME or rule trace per sample), per-feature (global feature importance), per-cohort (subgroup performance). At least the per-decision type is produced on demand for any consequential automated decision.

rationale.source: EU AI Act Article 13 (transparency); GDPR Article 22 (automated decision-making)

rule.rationale: A right-to-explanation request without typed explainability is a regulatory finding.

rule.statement: Human oversight is designed before deployment: queue path, escalation triggers, named reviewer roles, SLA. For each automated decision class, the policy is one of: pre-decision review, post-decision review with reversal window, sampled review, none-with-rationale.
rule.rationale: Unspecified oversight defaults to none; that fails high-risk audit.

rule.statement: Every inference call writes a structured audit log entry: {ts, model_version, input_hash, output_hash, decision, confidence, reviewer (if human-in-loop), retention_class}. Log is append-only and held per retention policy (≥7 years for high-risk).

rationale.source: EU AI Act Article 12; SOC 2 CC7; ISO 42001 §8.3

rule.rationale: Mutable or short-retention logs cannot evidence compliance during incident review or audit.

example.title: Versioned model card commit
example.code: models/cv-screening/MODEL_CARD.md @ v3.2.1 provenance: trained on dataset CVScreen-v4 (2025-11-01..2026-03-15) intended_use: rank applicants by job-fit; not auto-reject out_of_scope: candidates under 18; non-EN CVs metrics: top-5 accuracy 0.82 (CI ±0.04); demographic parity diff 0.07 (gender) oversight: post-rank human review of bottom 20%; reversal SLA 24h retention: audit-log 7 years

example.title: No card, no bias stats
example.code: shipped cv-screening v3.2.1; "we tested for bias and it looks fine"
example.note: Will not pass any external audit; right-to-explanation requests cannot be served.
